items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.

items.correct: Gradually increasing training demands so the body keeps adapting.
items.misconception: Training as hard as possible immediately.
items.why: Students think 'overload' means 'maximum'. It means slightly exceeding current capacity - too much causes injury; too little stalls progress. The key word is PROGRESSIVE.

items.q: 'This Girl Can' is which body's campaign?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Sport England. “UK Sport” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Olympism. “Functionalism” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Number of recommended weekly moderate-exercise minutes (UK, adults)?
items.why: The real figure is 150 minutes. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
